title: Components
description: Learn about different content block types

blocks.text: This wiki supports a variety of content blocks that you can use to create rich, well-structured documentation.

items.text: Callout boxes for alerts and notices
Cut blocks for collapsible content sections

blocks.text: This is an info callout. Use it to provide helpful information to your readers.
This is a warning callout. Use it to alert readers about important considerations.
This is a success callout. Use it to highlight positive outcomes or completed tasks.
This is an error callout. Use it to warn about errors or issues to avoid.
Cut blocks allow you to create collapsible sections to hide detailed content until the user needs it.

blocks.title: Click to expand: Basic Cut Example

blocks.text: This content is hidden by default and can be expanded by clicking the title. It's useful for hiding detailed information, long code examples, or optional content.

items.text: Reduces initial page load visual clutter
Allows users to focus on what they need

blocks.text: You can include any type of content inside a cut block, including code examples:
Steps blocks are perfect for creating step-by-step guides and tutorials. Each step is numbered in a circle and connected with a vertical dashed line.

items.title: Install Dependencies
items.description: First, install all required dependencies using your package manager. Run `npm install` or `pnpm install` in your project directory.

items.title: Configure Environment
items.description: Create a `.env.local` file and add your environment variables. Make sure to never commit sensitive data to version control.

items.title: Start Development Server
items.description: Run the development server with `npm run dev`. Your application will be available at `http://localhost:3000`.

items.title: Build for Production
items.description: When ready to deploy, build your application with `npm run build`. This creates an optimized production bundle.

blocks.text: Use headings to organize your content. The wiki supports h1 through h6 headings.
Paragraphs are the basic building blocks of text content. They support simple text formatting and are responsive on all screen sizes.
Both ordered and unordered lists are supported:
The wiki supports syntax-highlighted code blocks for many programming languages.
Create visual diagrams using Mermaid syntax. The wiki supports flowcharts, sequences, class diagrams, and more.

blocks.diagram:
flowchart TD
    A[Start] --> B{Is it working?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Great!]
    B -->|No| D[Debug]
    D --> B
    C --> E[End]

sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Browser
    participant Server
    User->>Browser: Click button
    Browser->>Server: Send request
    Server->>Server: Process data
    Server->>Browser: Send response
    Browser->>User: Display result
